key_insight: A coupled LSTM + physics-informed GNN can reconstruct history-dependent, non-linear local stress fields by separating temporal constitutive memory from spatial equilibrium-consistent field prediction.
engineering_relevance: This enables fast, mesh-agnostic surrogate simulation of elasto-plastic microstructures, reducing finite element cost by orders of magnitude while preserving field accuracy.
method: LSTM encodes macroscopic stress-strain loading history into a compact hidden state.
Physics-informed GNN/MeshGraphNet reconstructs spatial stress fields using mesh connectivity and equilibrium-based regularization.
A linear warm-up weighting balances reconstruction loss and divergence-based physics loss during training.
evidence_signal: Trained on 10,000 non-proportional loading paths for a plate-with-a-hole microstructure, the model achieved roughly 10^3× speedup over FE and 1.9% cumulative error on sequences twice the training length.
watch_item: Mesh-agnostic transfer is promising, but accuracy may still depend on how well the training data cover extreme loading paths, mesh resolutions, and constitutive regimes.
paper_title: Non-linear mechanical field reconstruction coupling recurrent neural networks with physics-informed graph neural networks
